mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in real consumption demand, proxied by rising retail sales, signals stronger near-term corporate revenue growth and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations, or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 1.5% while continuing to rise signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with a flat or falling unemployment rate signals durable income growth without labor-market deterioration, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0.0pp
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor conditions
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-flat-or-down condition has a lower mean 20-day S&P 500 return than all other observations, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening real output and margin pressure for cyclical firms, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: growth slowdown or industrial recession risk
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the downside return spread versus positive INDPRO changes is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in housing starts signals deteriorating household demand and weaker construction-linked activity, leading investors to mark down cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10% over 3 months are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than other periods, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a rising federal funds rate signals an inflation-driven tightening response that compresses valuation multiples faster than nominal earnings adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI-up and FEDFUNDS-up condition has nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 excess returns, or if the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment-rate increase above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a rapid labor-market deterioration that triggers defensive equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: early recession scare or labor-market inflection
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE jumps are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if median triggered return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP expansion accompanied by low policy rates signals a growth-supportive regime in which earnings expectations rise while discount-rate pressure remains limited, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter change is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% does not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods with FEDFUNDS at or above 2.0%, or if the conditional return spread is negative.
